% Nature Communications Paper Template
% Generated by ARIS paper-write skill
% Class file: download sn-jnl.cls from https://www.springernature.com/gp/authors/campaigns/latex-author-support
% Or use: https://www.overleaf.com/latex/templates/springer-nature-latex-template/gsvvftmrppwq

\documentclass[sn-nature]{sn-jnl}
% Options: sn-nature (Nature-branded), sn-basic (other Springer journals)

% Math
\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb,amsfonts,amsthm,mathtools}

% Typography
\usepackage{graphicx}
\usepackage{multirow}
\usepackage{booktabs}
\usepackage{xcolor}
\usepackage{subcaption}

% cleveref must be loaded AFTER hyperref (sn-jnl loads hyperref internally)
\usepackage[capitalize,noabbrev]{cleveref}

% Theorems
\theoremstyle{plain}
\newtheorem{theorem}{Theorem}[section]
\newtheorem{proposition}[theorem]{Proposition}
\newtheorem{lemma}[theorem]{Lemma}
\newtheorem{corollary}[theorem]{Corollary}
\theoremstyle{definition}
\newtheorem{definition}[theorem]{Definition}
\newtheorem{assumption}[theorem]{Assumption}
\theoremstyle{remark}
\newtheorem{remark}[theorem]{Remark}

\crefname{assumption}{Assumption}{Assumptions}
\Crefname{assumption}{Assumption}{Assumptions}

% Shared math commands
% NOTE: sn-jnl.cls treats _ specially in some contexts.
% Use \input{math_commands} (no underscores in filename) or wrap in \detokenize.
\input{math_commands}

%% Article metadata
\title[Article]{Paper Title Here}

%% Authors — fill in for submission (Nature Comms is NOT anonymous)
\author[1]{\fnm{First} \sur{Author}}
\author[1,2]{\fnm{Second} \sur{Author}}
\author*[2]{\fnm{Corresponding} \sur{Author}}

\affil[1]{\orgname{University One}, \orgdiv{Department of Physics}, \orgaddress{\city{City}, \country{Country}}}
\affil[2]{\orgname{University Two}, \orgdiv{Department of Physics}, \orgaddress{\city{City}, \country{Country}}}

\begin{document}

\maketitle

\begin{abstract}
\input{sections/0-abstract}
\end{abstract}

%% Nature Comms uses keyword list (optional)
% \begin{keywords}
% keyword1 \sep keyword2 \sep keyword3
% \end{keywords}

\input{sections/1-introduction}
\input{sections/2-results}
\input{sections/3-discussion}
\input{sections/4-methods}

%% === Required Statements (Nature Comms mandates these) ===

\section*{Data availability}
The data that support the findings of this study are available from the corresponding author upon reasonable request.
% Or: deposited in [repository] with accession code [XXX].

\section*{Code availability}
The code used in this study is available at \url{https://github.com/xxx/xxx}.

%% Optional
% \section*{Acknowledgements}
% We thank ...

% \section*{Author contributions}
% A.B. conceived the study. C.D. performed the experiments. All authors contributed to writing.

% \section*{Competing interests}
% The authors declare no competing interests.

\bibliography{references}

%% Supplementary Information (separate file in real submission, inline here for convenience)
\newpage
\appendix
\input{sections/A-appendix}

\end{document}
